policy_title: Rituximab Intravenous Products for Non-Oncology Indications

covered_indications.indication_name: ANCA-Associated Vasculitis – Induction Treatment
clinical_criteria: Patient has an ANCA-associated vasculitis (examples include granulomatosis with polyangiitis [Wegener's granulomatosis], or microscopic polyangiitis); AND
The medication is being administered in combination with glucocorticoids; AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a rheumatologist, nephrologist, pulmonologist, or immunologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: ANCA-Associated Vasculitis – Follow-Up Treatment of Patients Who Have Received Induction Treatment
clinical_criteria: According to the prescriber, the patient achieved disease control with induction treatment (includes a patient who received induction treatment using a rituximab product or other standard of care immunosuppressants); AND
If the patient previously received a course of therapy, at least 16 weeks will elapse between courses; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Pemphigus Vulgaris – Initial Treatment
clinical_criteria: Therapy is initiated in combination with a corticosteroid unless contraindicated (an example of a corticosteroid is prednisone); AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a dermatologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Pemphigus Vulgaris – Treatment of a Relapse
clinical_criteria: Subsequent infusions will be administered no sooner than 16 weeks following the previous infusion of a rituximab product (there will be a minimum of 16 weeks since the first dose of the previous course and the first dose of the next course of a rituximab product); AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a dermatologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Pemphigus Vulgaris – Maintenance
clinical_criteria: Subsequent infusions will be administered no sooner than 16 weeks following the previous infusion of a rituximab product (there will be a minimum of 16 weeks since the first dose of the previous course and the first dose of the next course of a rituximab product); AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a dermatologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Rheumatoid Arthritis – Initial Therapy
clinical_criteria: Patient has tried ONE conventional synthetic disease-modifying antirheumatic drug (DMARD) for at least 3 months (examples include methotrexate [oral or injectable], leflunomide, hydroxychloroquine, and sulfasalazine; an exception to the requirement for a trial of one conventional synthetic DMARD can be made if the patient already has a 3-month trial of at least one biologic other than the requested drug; a biosimilar of the requested biologic does not count; a patient who has already tried a biologic is not required to 'step back' and try a conventional synthetic DMARD); AND
The medication will not be used concurrently with another biologic or with a targeted synthetic DMARD; AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a rheumatologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Rheumatoid Arthritis – Patient has already received one course of a Rituximab Product
clinical_criteria: 16 weeks or greater will elapse between treatment courses (there will be a minimum of 16 weeks since the first dose of the previous course and the first dose of the next course of a rituximab product); AND
The medication will not be used concurrently with another biologic or with a targeted synthetic DMARD; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Rheumatoid Arthritis – Patient has already received two or more courses of a Rituximab Product
clinical_criteria: 16 weeks or greater will elapse between treatment courses (there will be a minimum of 16 weeks since the first dose of the previous course and the first dose of the next course of a rituximab product); AND
The medication will not be used concurrently with another biologic or with a targeted synthetic DMARD; AND

Patient meets at least ONE of the following: (a) Patient experienced a beneficial clinical response when assessed by at least one objective measure (examples of standardized and validated measures of disease activity include Clinical Disease Activity Index [CDAI], Disease Activity Score [DAS] 28 using erythrocyte sedimentation rate [ESR] or C-reactive protein [CRP], Patient Activity Scale [PAS]-II, Rapid Assessment of Patient Index Data 3 [RAPID-3], and/or Simplified Disease Activity Index [SDAI]); OR (b) Patient experienced an improvement in at least one symptom, such as decreased joint pain, morning stiffness, or fatigue; improved function or activities of daily living; decreased soft tissue swelling in joints or tendon sheaths; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia
clinical_criteria: The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a hematologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Graft-Versus-Host Disease – Initial Therapy
clinical_criteria: Patient has chronic graft-versus-host disease; AND

Patient has tried at least one systemic medication for graft-versus-host disease (examples of systemic medications include systemic corticosteroids [methylprednisolone, prednisone], Jakafi [ruxolitinib], Rezurock [belumosudil], Niktimvo [axatilimab-csfr], cyclosporine, tacrolimus, mycophenolate mofetil, Imbruvica [ibrutinib], imatinib, hydroxychloroquine, methotrexate, Nipent [pentostatin], interleukin-2 [e.g., Proleukin (aldesleukin)], sirolimus, or an etanercept product); AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with an oncologist, hematologist, or a physician affiliated with a transplant center; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Graft-Versus-Host Disease – Patient has Already Received a Course of a Rituximab Product
clinical_criteria: Patient meets ONE of the following: (a) When assessed by at least one objective measure, patient experienced a beneficial clinical response from baseline prior to initiating a rituximab product (examples of objective measures include normalization of liver function tests, red blood cell count, or platelet count; or resolution of fever or rash); OR (b) Compared with baseline prior to initiating a rituximab product, patient experienced an improvement in at least one symptom, such as improvement in skin, oral mucosal, ocular, or gastrointestinal symptoms (e.g., nausea, vomiting, anorexia); AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Hematopoietic Cell Transplantation
clinical_criteria: The medication will be used as part of a conditioning regimen for allogeneic transplant; AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with an oncologist, hematologist, or a physician affiliated with a transplant center; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Immune Thrombocytopenia (ITP) – Initial Therapy
clinical_criteria: Patient has tried one other therapy (examples of therapies for ITP include intravenous immunoglobulin [IVIG], anti-D [RHO] immunoglobulin, corticosteroids, Alvaiz [eltrombopag], Doptelet [avatrombopag], Nplate [romiplostim], Promacta [eltrombopag], Tavalisse [fostamatinib] and splenectomy); AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a hematologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Immune Thrombocytopenia (ITP) – Patient has Already Received a Course of a Rituximab Product
clinical_criteria: At least 6 months will elapse between treatment courses (there will be a minimum of 6 months separating the first dose of the previous course and the first dose of the requested course of a rituximab product); AND
According to the prescriber, the patient responded to therapy (examples of a response include a platelet count increase from baseline following treatment with a rituximab product); AND
According to the prescriber, the patient has relapsed (examples of relapse include the patient experiences thrombocytopenia after achievement of a remission); AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities Associated with Checkpoint Inhibitors – Initial Therapy
clinical_criteria: According to the prescriber, patient developed an immunotherapy-related toxicity; AND

Patient developed this immunotherapy-related toxicity while receiving a checkpoint inhibitor (examples include Keytruda [pembrolizumab], Opdivo [nivolumab], Yervoy [ipilimumab], Tecentriq [atezolizumab], Bavencio [avelumab], Imfinzi [durvalumab], and Libtayo [cemiplimab-rwlc]); AND
Patient is symptomatic despite a trial of at least ONE systemic corticosteroid (examples include methylprednisolone and prednisone); AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with an oncologist, hematologist, nephrologist, neurologist, rheumatologist, or dermatologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities Associated with Checkpoint Inhibitors – Patient has Already Received a Course of a Rituximab Product
clinical_criteria: The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with an oncologist, hematologist, nephrologist, neurologist, rheumatologist, or dermatologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Interstitial Lung Disease Associated with Systemic Autoimmune Rheumatic Disease – Initial Therapy
clinical_criteria: Patient is ≥ 18 years of age; AND
Diagnosis is confirmed by high-resolution computed tomography; AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a pulmonologist or a rheumatologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Interstitial Lung Disease Associated with Systemic Autoimmune Rheumatic Disease – Patient has Already Received a Course of a Rituximab Product
clinical_criteria: 24 weeks or greater will elapse between treatment courses (there will be a minimum of 24 weeks since the first dose of the previous course and the first dose of the next course of a rituximab product); AND

Patient has experienced a beneficial response to therapy with rituximab (examples of a beneficial response include a reduction in the anticipated decline in forced vital capacity, improvement in 6-minute walk distance, and/or reduction in the number or severity of disease-related exacerbations); AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a pulmonologist or a rheumatologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Membranous Nephropathy – Initial Therapy
clinical_criteria: According to the prescriber, the patient is at moderate risk or high risk for the progressive loss of kidney function; AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a nephrologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Membranous Nephropathy – Patient has Already Received a Course of a Rituximab Product
clinical_criteria: The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a nephrologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Minimal Change Disease – Initial Therapy
clinical_criteria: The medication is being used for frequently relapsing or steroid-dependent disease; AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a nephrologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Minimal Change Disease – Patient has Already Received a Course of a Rituximab Product
clinical_criteria: The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a nephrologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Multiple Sclerosis – Initial Therapy
clinical_criteria: According to the prescriber, the patient has experienced inadequate efficacy or significant intolerance to at least TWO other disease-modifying agents for multiple sclerosis (see Appendix B for examples); AND
Medication will not be used concurrently with another disease-modifying agent used for multiple sclerosis; AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a neurologist or a physician who specializes in the treatment of multiple sclerosis; AND

At least 6 months will elapse between treatment courses (if the patient has already received a course of therapy there will be a minimum of 6 months separating the first dose of the previous course and the first dose of the requested course of therapy); AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Multiple Sclerosis – Patient is Currently Receiving Rituximab for less than 1 year
clinical_criteria: Medication will not be used concurrently with another disease-modifying agent used for multiple sclerosis; AND

At least 6 months will elapse between treatment courses (there will be a minimum of 6 months separating the first dose of the previous course and the first dose of the requested course of therapy); AND
Medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a neurologist or a physician who specializes in the treatment of multiple sclerosis; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Multiple Sclerosis – Patient is Currently Receiving Rituximab for 1 year or more
clinical_criteria: Medication will not be used concurrently with another disease-modifying agent used for multiple sclerosis; AND

At least 6 months will elapse between treatment courses (there will be a minimum of 6 months separating the first dose of the previous course and the first dose of the requested course of therapy); AND

Patient meets ONE of the following: (1) Patient experienced a beneficial clinical response when assessed by at least one objective measure (examples include stabilization or reduced worsening in disease activity as evaluated by MRI [absence or a decrease in gadolinium enhancing lesions, decrease in the number of new or enlarging T2 lesions]; stabilization or reduced worsening on the Expanded Disability Status Scale [EDSS] score; achievement in criteria for No Evidence of Disease Activity-3 [NEDA-3] or NEDA-4; improvement on the fatigue symptom and impact questionnaire-relapsing multiple sclerosis [FSIQ-RMS] scale; reduction or absence of relapses; improvement or maintenance on the six-minute walk test or 12-Items Multiple Sclerosis Walking Scale; improvement on the Multiple Sclerosis Functional Composite [MSFC] score; and/or attenuation of brain volume loss); OR (2) Patient experienced stabilization, slow progression, or improvement in at least one symptom such as motor function, fatigue, vision, bowel/bladder function, spasticity, walking/gait, or pain/numbness/tingling sensation; AND
Medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a neurologist or a physician who specializes in the treatment of multiple sclerosis; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Myasthenia Gravis – Initial Therapy
clinical_criteria: Patient has confirmed anti-muscle-specific tyrosine kinase antibody-positive myasthenia gravis; AND

Patient meets ONE of the following: (a) Patient previously received or is currently receiving pyridostigmine; OR (b) Patient has had inadequate efficacy, contraindication, or significant intolerance to pyridostigmine; AND

Patient has tried at least one immunosuppressant therapy (examples include azathioprine, cyclosporine, mycophenolate mofetil, methotrexate, tacrolimus, and cyclophosphamide; a trial of Imaavy [nipocalimab-aahu intravenous infusion] or Rystiggo [rozanolixizumab-noli subcutaneous infusion] also counts); AND

Patient has evidence of unresolved symptoms of myasthenia gravis (includes difficulty swallowing, difficulty breathing, or a functional disability resulting in the discontinuation of physical activity [e.g., double vision, talking, impairment of mobility]); AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a neurologist; AND
Preferred Product Criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Myasthenia Gravis – Patient has Already Received a Course of a Rituximab Product
clinical_criteria: According to the prescriber, patient is continuing to derive benefit from the rituximab product (examples of benefit include reductions in exacerbations of myasthenia gravis; improvements in speech, swallowing, mobility, and respiratory function); AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a neurologist; AND
Preferred Product Criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder
clinical_criteria: Medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a neurologist; AND
Preferred Product Criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Pediatric Nephrotic Syndrome – Initial Therapy
clinical_criteria: Patient is ≤ 18 years of age; AND

Patient meets ONE of the following: (a) Patient has tried at least one systemic corticosteroid (examples include prednisone or prednisolone); OR (b) Patient has tried at least one glucocorticoid-sparing agent for nephrotic syndrome (examples include oral calcineurin inhibitors [e.g., tacrolimus, cyclosporine], cyclophosphamide, or mycophenolate mofetil); AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a nephrologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Pediatric Nephrotic Syndrome – Patient has Already Received a Course of a Rituximab Product
clinical_criteria: The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a nephrologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Solid Organ Transplantation
clinical_criteria: Patient meets ONE of the following: (i) The medication will be used for desensitization therapy prior to or immediately after transplantation; OR (ii) The medication will be used for antibody-mediated rejection; AND
The medication will be prescribed by or in consultation with a physician affiliated with a transplant center; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) [Lupus] – Initial Therapy
clinical_criteria: This includes nephrotic syndrome in a patient with SLE.

Patient has tried at least ONE standard immunomodulating or immunosuppressant agent (examples include hydroxychloroquine, corticosteroids [e.g., prednisone, methylprednisolone], methotrexate, azathioprine, mycophenolate, and cyclophosphamide); AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a rheumatologist, nephrologist, or neurologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) [Lupus] – Individual has Already Received a Course of a Rituximab Product
clinical_criteria: 6 months or greater will elapse between treatment courses (there will be a minimum of 6 months separating the first dose of the previous rituximab course and the first dose of the requested course of rituximab); AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

covered_indications.indication_name: Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic Purpura
clinical_criteria: The medication will be used in combination with systemic corticosteroids (examples include prednisone and methylprednisolone); AND
The medication will be used in combination with therapeutic plasma exchange; AND
The medication is prescribed by or in consultation with a hematologist; AND
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

required_prior_drugs: sulfasalazine (one conventional synthetic DMARD for at least 3 months, OR one biologic other than the requested drug for at least 3 months)
step_therapy.condition_description: Rheumatoid Arthritis – Initial Therapy: patient must have tried a conventional synthetic DMARD prior to rituximab, unless the patient has already tried a biologic.
Immune Thrombocytopenia (ITP) – Initial Therapy: patient must have tried one other therapy for ITP.
Graft-Versus-Host Disease – Initial Therapy: patient must have tried at least one systemic medication for GVHD.
Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities – Initial Therapy: patient must be symptomatic despite a trial of at least ONE systemic corticosteroid.

required_prior_drugs: at least TWO disease-modifying agents for multiple sclerosis (see Appendix B for examples)
step_therapy.condition_description: Multiple Sclerosis – Initial Therapy: patient must have experienced inadequate efficacy or significant intolerance to at least TWO other disease-modifying agents for multiple sclerosis.
Myasthenia Gravis – Initial Therapy: patient must have tried at least one immunosuppressant therapy and must have received or tried pyridostigmine.
Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) – Initial Therapy: patient must have tried at least ONE standard immunomodulating or immunosuppressant agent.
Pediatric Nephrotic Syndrome – Initial Therapy: patient must have tried at least one systemic corticosteroid OR at least one glucocorticoid-sparing agent.

Rituxan (rituximab) preferred product step therapy: patient must have tried all biosimilar alternatives (Truxima, Riabni, Ruxience) before Rituxan will be approved, unless a formulation difference would cause a significant allergy or serious adverse reaction.

excluded_indications: Rituximab products (Rituxan, Riabni, Ruxience, Truxima) for any other non-oncology use is considered not medically necessary.
Oncology indications (including post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder and Castleman's disease) are addressed in a separate coverage policy (Oncology Medications #1403).
Rituxan Hycela (rituximab and hyaluronidase human) is addressed in a separate coverage policy.
Factor Inhibitors in an Individual with Hemophilia – this use was removed from this policy.
approval_duration_initial: Varies by indication: 1 month for most initial therapy approvals; 6 months for Myasthenia Gravis; 1 year for Solid Organ Transplantation and Multiple Sclerosis initial therapy.
approval_duration_renewal: Varies by indication: 1 month for most renewal/subsequent course approvals; 1 year for ANCA-Associated Vasculitis follow-up, GVHD subsequent course, Multiple Sclerosis (≥1 year on rituximab), Pemphigus Vulgaris maintenance, and Solid Organ Transplantation.

general_requirements: Prior Authorization is required for benefit coverage of rituximab IV products.
Approval is recommended for those who meet the Criteria and Dosing for the listed indications.
Requests for doses outside of the established dosing documented in this policy will be considered on a case-by-case basis by a clinician (i.e., Medical Director or Pharmacist).
In cases where the approval is authorized in months, 1 month is equal to 30 days.
Initial approval requires rituximab to be prescribed by or in consultation with a physician who specializes in the condition being treated.

Documentation is required where noted in the criteria as [documentation required]. Documentation may include, but is not limited to, chart notes, laboratory tests, claims records, and/or other information. All documentation must include patient-specific identifying information.

Employer Plans – Rituxan (rituximab intravenous infusion) Product Criteria: Patient meets BOTH of the following (A and B): A. Patient has tried ALL of the following: Truxima, Riabni, and Ruxience [documentation required]; B. Patient cannot continue to use each formulary alternative due to a formulation difference in the inactive ingredient(s) [e.g., differences in stabilizing agent, buffering agent, and/or surfactant] which, according to the prescriber, would result in a significant allergy or serious adverse reaction [documentation required].

Individual and Family Plans – Rituxan (rituximab intravenous infusion) Product Criteria: Patient meets BOTH of the following (A and B): A. Patient has tried ALL of the following: Truxima, Riabni, and Ruxience [documentation required]; B. Patient cannot continue to use each formulary alternative due to a formulation difference in the inactive ingredient(s) [e.g., differences in stabilizing agent, buffering agent, and/or surfactant] which, according to the prescriber, would result in a significant allergy or serious adverse reaction [documentation required].
policy_changes: 7/1/2025 – Selected Revision: Preferencing Product Table. Removed 'Individual has previously started on or is currently receiving Rituxan (rituximab)'.

8/15/2025 – Selected Revision: Added documentation instructions. Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder: Updated from 'Documented diagnosis of neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder' to 'diagnosis was confirmed by a positive blood serum test for anti-aquaporin-4 antibody [documentation required]'.

10/15/2025 – Annual Revision: Policy Title updated from 'Rituximab for Non-Oncology Indication' to 'Rituximab Intravenous Products for Non-Oncology Indications'. ANCA-Associated Vasculitis: Induction Therapy approval duration updated from 12 months to 1 month; removed Churg-Strauss syndrome and pauci-immune glomerulonephritis from examples; updated glucocorticoid requirement; added pulmonologist to specialty requirement. Follow-Up Treatment: updated prescriber language; updated interval language; removed specialty requirement. Dosing: updated adult and pediatric follow-up dosing language. Pemphigus Vulgaris: renamed indication; updated approval durations; added relapse-specific and maintenance-specific criteria; updated dosing. Rheumatoid Arthritis: updated approval durations; added criteria for two or more courses. Graft-Versus-Host Disease: updated approval duration; added chronic GVHD criterion; updated prior therapy language; added criteria for subsequent course. Hematopoietic Cell Transplantation: added criteria and dosing. Immune Thrombocytopenia: updated name; updated approval duration; updated prior therapy language; updated subsequent course language; removed hematologist requirement for subsequent course. Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities: added checkpoint inhibitor examples; updated approval durations; added prescriber attestation criteria; added specialties; updated dosing. Multiple Sclerosis: updated prior therapy requirement from one to two agents; added criteria for currently receiving rituximab. Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder: updated approval duration from 12 months to 1 month; removed AQP4 antibody documentation requirement. Systemic Lupus Erythematosus: corrected spelling; updated approval durations; updated prior therapy language; updated subsequent course criteria; added dosing.

2/1/2026 – Selected Revision: Pemphigus Vulgaris Maintenance Therapy: Added 'every 6 months' to dosing regimen. Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia: updated name from 'Refractory Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia'; updated authorization duration from 12 months to 1 month; removed prior therapy failure requirement; added hematologist requirement; added dosing. Immune Thrombocytopenia (ITP): updated subsequent course response/relapse language from 'as determined by the prescriber' to 'according to the prescriber'. Interstitial Lung Disease Associated with Systemic Autoimmune Rheumatic Disease: added criteria and dosing. Membranous Nephropathy: updated name; updated authorization duration from 12 months to 1 month; removed specific risk criteria; added moderate/high risk language; added subsequent course criteria; added dosing. Minimal Change Disease: added criteria and dosing. Myasthenia Gravis: updated authorization duration from 12 months to 6 months; added anti-MuSK antibody criterion; added pyridostigmine criterion; updated prior therapy requirement; added unresolved symptoms criterion; added neurologist requirement; added subsequent course criteria; added dosing. Pediatric Nephrotic Syndrome: updated authorization duration from 12 months to 1 month; updated prior therapy language; added nephrologist requirement; added subsequent course criteria; added dosing. Solid Organ Transplantation: updated desensitization language; updated antibody-mediated rejection language; added transplant center affiliation requirement; added dosing. Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic Purpura: updated authorization duration from 12 months to 1 month; removed diagnosis criterion; updated corticosteroid requirement language; updated plasma exchange language; added dosing. Factor Inhibitors in an Individual with Hemophilia: this use was removed.

--- Page 1 ---
Drug Coverage Policy
Effective Date .................. 02/01/2026
Coverage Policy Number .......... IP0319
Policy Title….... Rituximab Intravenous
Products for Non-Oncology Indications
Rituximab Intravenous Products for Non-
Oncology Indications
• Riabni™ (rituximab-arrx intravenous infusion – Amgen)
• Rituxan® (rituximab intravenous infusion − Genentech)
• Ruxience® (rituximab-pvvr intravenous infusion – Pfizer)
• Truxima® (rituximab-abbs intravenous infusion – Celltrion/Teva)
INSTRUCTIONS FOR USE
The following Coverage Policy applies to health benefit plans administered by Cigna Companies.
Certain Cigna Companies and/or lines of business only provide utilization review services to clients
and do not make coverage determinations. References to standard benefit plan language and
coverage determinations do not apply to those clients. Coverage Policies are intended to provide
guidance in interpreting certain standard benefit plans administered by Cigna Companies. Please
note, the terms of a customer’s particular benefit plan document [Group Service Agreement,
Evidence of Coverage, Certificate of Coverage, Summary Plan Description (SPD) or similar plan
document] may differ significantly from the standard benefit plans upon which these Coverage
Policies are based. For example, a customer’s benefit plan document may contain a specific

OVERVIEW
Rituximab products are CD20-directed cytolytic antibodies. All approved rituximab intravenous
products are indicated for treatment of the following conditions:1-3,22
• Chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL), in combination with fludarabine and
cyclophosphamide (FC) for the treatment of patients with previously untreated and
previously treated CD20-positive disease.
• Granulomatosis with polyangiitis (Wegener’s granulomatosis) and microscopic
polyangiitis in adults, in combination with glucocorticoids.
• Non-Hodgkin lymphoma (NHL), for the following uses:
previously untreated follicular, CD20-positive disease, in combination with first-line
o
chemotherapy, and in patients achieving a complete or partial response to rituximab in
combination with chemotherapy, as a single-agent maintenance therapy.
for relapsed or refractory, low-grade or follicular, CD20-positive, B-cell disease.
o
for non-progressing (including stable disease) low-grade, CD20-positive, B-cell disease
o
as a single agent after first-line cyclophosphamide/vincristine/prednisone (CVP)
chemotherapy.
for previously untreated diffuse large B-cell, CD20-positive disease, in combination with
o
cyclophosphamide/doxorubicin/vincristine/prednisone (CHOP) or other anthracycline-
based chemotherapy regimens.
• Pemphigus vulgaris, for adults with moderate to severe disease.
• Rheumatoid arthritis, in adult patients with moderately to severely active disease, in
combination with methotrexate for patients who have had an inadequate response to one
or more tumor necrosis factor inhibitors.
In addition to the above indications, Rituxan intravenous is also indicated for treatment of the
following conditions:1
• Granulomatosis with polyangiitis (Wegener’s granulomatosis) and microscopic
polyangiitis in patients ≥ 2 years of age, in combination with glucocorticoids.
• B-cell lymphoma, in patients ≥ 6 months of age with previously untreated, advanced
stage, CD20-positive diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, Burkitt lymphoma, Burkitt-like
lymphoma, or mature B-cell acute leukemia in combination with chemotherapy.
Riabni, Ruxience, and Truxima are approved as biosimilars to Rituxan intravenous, indicating no
clinically meaningful differences in safety and effectiveness and the same mechanism of action,
route of administration, dosage form, and strength as Rituxan intravenous. However, minor
differences in clinically inactive components are allowed. At this time, biosimilars have not
demonstrated interchangeability.
Guidelines
The use of rituximab is supported in clinical guidelines in numerous situations, both as first-line
therapy and in patients who have refractory disease or have relapsed following treatment with
other therapies.4-21
• Antineutrophil Cytoplasmic Antibody (ANCA)-Associated Vasculitis: Guidelines
from the American College of Rheumatology (ACR) [2021] list rituximab among the
alternatives for induction or maintenance of remission. Various regimens are recommended
with a typical maximum of 1,000 mg/infusion. For maintenance dosing, at least 4 months
should separate doses. The optimal dose of rituximab for remission maintenance remains
uncertain. Although scheduled maintenance is conditionally recommended over the use of

--- Page 3 ---
CD19+ B-cell counts and/or ANCA titers to guide retreatment, there are data to support
both approaches.
• Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia (AIHA): AIHA is divided serologically into warm type
(65% of cases), cold type (29% of cases are cold hemagglutinin disease [CHAD]),
paroxysmal cold hemoglobinuria (1% of cases), or mixed AIHA (5% of cases).30 For
primary warm AIHA, prednisolone is recommended first-line by the British Society for
Haematology (BSH) with rituximab as second-line if no response or if patient relapses.
Third-line options include azathioprine, cyclosporin, danazol, mycophenolate mofetil, or
splenectomy. For CHAD, rituximab is considered a first-line therapy. Mixed AIHA is treated
as warm AIHA. Supportive care is recommended for paroxysmal cold hemoglobinuria. The
BSH guidelines note that the standard regimen for rituximab IV is 375 mg/m2 weekly for 4
consecutive weeks; however low dose rituximab (i.e., 100 mg weekly for 4 weeks with
prednisolone, first or second line) produced comparable response rates.
• Immune Thrombocytopenia (ITP): Guidelines from the American Society of
Hematology for ITP (2019) mention rituximab as an alternative for children and adults with
ITP who do not respond to first-line treatment, and for adults who are corticosteroid-
dependent.17
• Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities Associated with Checkpoint Inhibitors: NCCN
(version 1.2025 – December 20, 2024) and the American Society of Clinical Oncology
(ASCO) guidelines (2021) recommend rituximab as an option for corticosteroid-refractory
dermatologic and hematologic immune mediated adverse events, as well as for myasthenia
gravis, immune-mediated encephalitis, myositis, and stage 3 acute kidney injury/elevated
serum creatinine.26,27
• Interstitial Lung Disease Associated with Systemic Autoimmune Rheumatic
Disease (SARD-ILD): The American College of Rheumatology (ACR) and the American
College of Chest Physicians (CHEST) [2023] conditionally recommend rituximab as a first-
line ILD treatment option for adults with SARD-ILD.48 Other first-line options include
mycophenolate, azathioprine, and cyclophosphamide. In addition, rituximab is conditionally
recommended for people with SARD-ILD progression despite first ILD treatment. Rituximab
is also conditionally recommended for people with SARD and rapidly progressive ILD as a
first-line treatment option. Other first-line treatment options include cyclophosphamide,
intravenous immune globulin, mycophenolate, a calcineurin inhibitor, and Janus kinases
inhibitors. Recommended dosing is 1 gram IV every 2 weeks for 2 doses; treatment may
be repeated every 24 weeks as needed.
• Membranous Nephropathy: The Kidney Disease: Improving Global Outcomes (KDIGO)
Clinical Practice Guideline for the Management of Glomerular Diseases (2021) list rituximab
as a therapeutic option for membranous nephropathy in patients at moderate risk or high
risk for progressive loss of kidney function.31 In patients who relapse, initial therapy can
be repeated or treatment may be switched to rituximab if the initial treatment was a
calcineurin inhibitor or cyclophosphamide. KDIGO recommends a treatment regimen of 1
or 2 infusions of 1 gram of rituximab each administered 2 weeks apart or 375 mg/m2 give
1-4 times at weekly intervals.
• Minimal Change Disease (MCD): The Kidney Disease: Improving Global Outcomes
(KDIGO) Clinical Practice Guideline for the Management of Glomerular Diseases (2021) list
rituximab as a therapeutic option for adults with frequently relapsing or steroid-dependent
MCD.31 KDIGO recommends one of the following induction regimens: 375 mg/m2 weekly

for 4 doses; 375 mg/m2 for a single dose and repeat after one week if CD19 cells >
5/mm3; or 1 gram/dose for 2 doses, 2 weeks apart. For relapses after induction, either
375 mg/m2 for one dose or 1 gram for 1 dose is recommended.
• Multiple Sclerosis (MS): In June 2019, a consensus paper was updated by the MS
Coalition that discusses the use of disease-modifying therapies in MS.18 Rituximab is listed
among various options, involving different mechanisms of action and modes of
administration, which have shown benefits in patients with MS. The American Academy of
Neurology has practice guidelines regarding disease-modifying therapies for adults with
MS.19 The guidelines mention rituximab for use in MS.
• Myasthenia Gravis (MG): An international consensus guidance for the management of
MG was published in 2016.35 The consensus guidance recommends pyridostigmine for the
initial treatment in most patients with MG. The ability to discontinue pyridostigmine can
indicate that the patient has met treatment goals and may guide the tapering of other
therapies. Corticosteroids or immunosuppressant therapy should be used in all patients
with MG who have not met treatment goals after an adequate trial of pyridostigmine.
Nonsteroidal immunosuppressant agents used in MG include azathioprine, cyclosporine,
mycophenolate mofetil, methotrexate, and tacrolimus. It is usually necessary to maintain
some immunosuppression for many years, sometimes for life. Plasma exchange and
intravenous immunoglobulin can be used as short-term treatments for certain patients. An
update to an international consensus guidance for the management of MG was published in
2020.36 Rituximab should be considered as an early therapeutic option in patients with
muscle specific kinase antibody-positive MG who have an unsatisfactory response to initial
immunotherapy. While the efficacy of rituximab in acetylcholine receptor antibody-positive
MG is uncertain, it is an option if patients fail or do not tolerate other immunosuppressive
agents. Various dosing regimens have been utilized in both prospective trials and
retrospective analyses that are referenced in the consensus guidance.
• Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorders (NMOSD): The Neuromyelitis Optica Study
Group (NEMOS) published revised recommendations for the treatment of NMOSD in 2023
and recommend rituximab as a treatment option for aquaporin-4 (AQP4)-immunoglobulin
G (IgG) positive NMOSD and double-negative NMOSD.20

o

o

o

o

• Pediatric Nephrotic Syndrome: The Kidney Disease: Improving Global Outcomes
(KDIGO) 2025 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Management of Nephrotic Syndrome in
Children recommends rituximab as a treatment option for steroid-sensitive nephrotic
syndrome in children who have frequent relapses despite optimal combinations of
prednisone and glucocorticoid-sparing oral agents.32 For steroid-resistant nephrotic
syndrome, KDIGO recommends cyclosporine or tacrolimus as initial second-line therapy. A

• Pemphigus Vulgaris: British guidelines (2017) list rituximab in combination with
corticosteroids as a first-line therapy.23
• Rheumatoid Arthritis: Guidelines from ACR (2021) recommend the addition of a biologic
(which includes rituximab) or a targeted synthetic disease modifying antirheumatic drug
(DMARD) for a patient taking the maximum tolerated dose of methotrexate who is not at

• Solid Organ Transplantation: Various transplant center protocols and clinical practice
guidelines discuss the use of rituximab for highly sensitized patients as part of
desensitization protocols and for acute antibody-mediated rejection following
transplantation.37-44
• Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE): European League Against Rheumatism
recommendations for the management of SLE (2023) mention rituximab as a therapeutic
option for patients who are refractory to standard immunosuppressive therapies.21
• Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic Purpura (TTP): The International Society on
Thrombosis and Haemostasis (ISTH) [2025] recommends the addition of rituximab to
corticosteroids and therapeutic plasma exchange for patients experiencing their first acute
event or relapses of immune-mediated TTP.33-34 This is a conditional recommendation in
the context of very low certainty of evidence. A dosing regimen of rituximab IV 375 mg/m2
administered weekly for 4 doses has been used in a phase II study and observed in
retrospective analyses.45-47
This coverage policy addresses the use of rituximab for non-oncology indications. The use of
rituximab for oncology indications (including post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder and
Castleman’s disease) and Rituxan Hycela® (rituximab and hyaluronidase human) are addressed in
a separate coverage policy. Please refer to the related coverage policy (Oncology Medications
#1403).

POLICY STATEMENT
Prior Authorization is required for benefit coverage of rituximab IV products. Approval is
recommended for those who meet the Criteria and Dosing for the listed indications. Requests for
doses outside of the established dosing documented in this policy will be considered on a case-by-
case basis by a clinician (i.e., Medical Director or Pharmacist). All approvals are provided for the
duration noted below. In cases where the approval is authorized in months, 1 month is equal to
30 days. Because of the specialized skills required for evaluation and diagnosis of patients treated
with rituximab products as well as the monitoring required for adverse events and long-term
efficacy, initial approval requires rituximab to be prescribed by or in consultation with a physician
who specializes in the condition being treated.
Documentation: Documentation is required where noted in the criteria as [documentation
required]. Documentation may include, but is not limited to, chart notes, laboratory tests, claims
records, and/or other information. All documentation must include patient-specific identifying
information.
